holdings.infoBox: DJIA is an equity ETF with a total of 33 individual holdings. The top holdings are The Goldman Sachs Group stock at 13.49%, Caterpillar at 11.73%, UnitedHealth Group at 4.90%, Microsoft at 4.65%, and Amgen at 4.19%.

overview.description: The Global X Dow 30 Covered Call ETF (DJIA) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the DJIA Cboe BuyWrite v2 index. The fund tracks an index that uses a covered-call strategy to provide long exposure to the stocks in the Dow Jones Industrial Average and sell at-the-money index call options on each position. DJIA was launched on Feb 23, 2022 and is issued by Global X.

data.text: Stocks ended the week higher as investors looked past policy noise and leaned back into risk assets. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) rose 0.70% to 51,202.26, while the S&P…

data.text: Stocks ended the week sharply lower as a stronger-than-expected May jobs report pushed bond yields higher and hit the market’s biggest winners. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) fell 1.35%…

data.title: These ETFs will give you high income — but you need to learn about their strategies first
data.text: Exchange-traded funds using options to generate income have become popular. They take a variety of approaches to providing income and growth.

data.title: Volatility got you nervous? These ETFs promise stock gains and plunge protection
data.text: If July's downside volatility has got you worried, here's the bad news: there could be more downside ahead. August, September and October are typically the worst months for U.S. stocks.

data.title: Should you buy DJIA ahead of Q4 2023? Hint: yes
data.text: Stocks have had a challenging year so far. After all, the Fed's tightening cycle made it difficult for investors to hold on to their portfolios and not flee for safer assets.

data.title: 4 reasons to start buying the August stock-market weakness — and what to buy
data.text: It's time to start buying the August stock-market weakness. But keep some purchasing power on hand to take advantage of weaker prices ahead.

data.title: Did stocks bottom? DJIA price forecast for Q4 2022
data.text: With only two trading days left in the month, stocks bounced from their 2022 lows. It may well be just a bounce and nothing more, but it coincides with the turnaround Tuesday bias, as explained here.
